Here is the complete SQL script you can run in the Supabase SQL Editor to create all the necessary tables and insert the initial required data. This schema is designed based on the application's current functionality.

### **SQL Setup Script for Supabase**

You can execute this entire script at once in your Supabase project's SQL Editor (`Database` > `SQL Editor`).

```sql
-- =================================================================
-- 1. USERS TABLE
-- Stores all user information for students, teachers, and admins.
-- =================================================================
CREATE TABLE public.users (
    id uuid NOT NULL DEFAULT gen_random_uuid() PRIMARY KEY,
    created_at timestamp with time zone NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
    name text,
    username text UNIQUE,
    email text,
    "password" text,
    "role" text,
    class_level text,
    assigned_classes text[],
    assigned_subjects text[],
    is_approved boolean DEFAULT false,
    avatar_url text,
    bio text,
    expertise text,
    reason_for_joining text,
    subscription_expiry timestamp with time zone,
    phone_number text,
    school_name text
);

-- =================================================================
-- 2. NOTES TABLE
-- Stores educational notes created by teachers or admins.
-- =================================================================
CREATE TABLE public.notes (
    id uuid NOT NULL DEFAULT gen_random_uuid() PRIMARY KEY,
    created_at timestamp with time zone NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
    subject text,
    title text,
    content text,
    upload_date timestamp with time zone,
    class_level text,
    is_archived boolean DEFAULT false,
    author_id uuid REFERENCES public.users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE
);

-- =================================================================
-- 3. ASSIGNMENTS TABLE
-- Stores assignments created by teachers or admins.
-- =================================================================
CREATE TABLE public.assignments (
    id uuid NOT NULL DEFAULT gen_random_uuid() PRIMARY KEY,
    created_at timestamp with time zone NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
    subject text,
    content text,
    due_date timestamp with time zone,
    posted_date timestamp with time zone,
    class_level text,
    is_archived boolean DEFAULT false,
    author_id uuid REFERENCES public.users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE
);

-- =================================================================
-- 4. DRAFTS TABLE
-- Stores auto-saved drafts of notes for users.
-- =================================================================
CREATE TABLE public.drafts (
    id uuid NOT NULL DEFAULT gen_random_uuid() PRIMARY KEY,
    user_id uuid NOT NULL REFERENCES public.users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    subject text,
    title text,
    content text,
    class_level text,
    updated_at timestamp with time zone DEFAULT now()
);


-- =================================================================
-- 5. ANNOUNCEMENTS TABLE
-- Stores announcements broadcast to students.
-- =================================================================
CREATE TABLE public.announcements (
    id uuid NOT NULL DEFAULT gen_random_uuid() PRIMARY KEY,
    created_at timestamp with time zone NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
    message text,
    sender text,
    date timestamp with time zone,
    class_level text
);

-- =================================================================
-- 6. QUESTIONS TABLE
-- Stores questions from students and answers from teachers/admins.
-- =================================================================
CREATE TABLE public.questions (
    id uuid NOT NULL DEFAULT gen_random_uuid() PRIMARY KEY,
    created_at timestamp with time zone NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
    student_id uuid REFERENCES public.users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    student_name text,
    question_text text,
    answer_text text,
    question_date timestamp with time zone,
    answer_date timestamp with time zone,
    is_answered boolean DEFAULT false
);

-- =================================================================
-- 7. FEEDBACK TABLE
-- Stores feedback submissions from students.
-- =================================================================
CREATE TABLE public.feedback (
    id uuid NOT NULL DEFAULT gen_random_uuid() PRIMARY KEY,
    created_at timestamp with time zone NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
    student_id uuid REFERENCES public.users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    student_name text,
    "type" text,
    subject text,
    message text,
    date timestamp with time zone
);

-- =================================================================
-- 8. PAYMENTS TABLE
-- Logs all payment transactions.
-- =================================================================
CREATE TABLE public.payments (
    id uuid NOT NULL DEFAULT gen_random_uuid() PRIMARY KEY,
    created_at timestamp with time zone NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
    user_id uuid REFERENCES public.users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    transaction_id text,
    amount numeric,
    status text,
    date timestamp with time zone
);

-- =================================================================
-- 9. SYSTEM_SETTINGS TABLE
-- A key-value store for global application settings.
-- =================================================================
CREATE TABLE public.system_settings (
    key text NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,
    value text
);

-- =================================================================
-- 10. INITIAL DATA
-- Insert default values into the system_settings table.
-- =================================================================
INSERT INTO public.system_settings (key, value) VALUES
    ('maintenance_mode', 'false'),
    ('maintenance_end_time', ''),
    ('student_fee', '10000'),
    ('teacher_fee', '50000'),
    ('school_fee', '300000')
ON CONFLICT (key) DO UPDATE SET value = EXCLUDED.value;

```

### **Important Next Steps**

After running the SQL script, there are a few more crucial steps to ensure your application works correctly and securely.

#### 1. Enable Row Level Security (RLS)

RLS is a fundamental security feature in Supabase. It ensures that users can only access data they are permitted to see.

Go to `Authentication` > `Policies` in your Supabase project and enable RLS for each table you created. Then, add policies. Here are some essential policies to get you started:

**Example: `notes` Table Policies**

```sql
-- 1. Allow authenticated users to read all notes
-- The app's frontend logic filters notes by class level for students.
CREATE POLICY "Allow authenticated read access"
ON public.notes
FOR SELECT
USING (auth.role() = 'authenticated');

-- 2. Allow teachers and admins to create notes
CREATE POLICY "Allow insert for admins and teachers"
ON public.notes
FOR INSERT
WITH CHECK (
  (SELECT role FROM public.users WHERE id = auth.uid()) IN ('admin', 'teacher')
);

-- 3. Allow authors to update their own notes
CREATE POLICY "Allow authors to update their own notes"
ON public.notes
FOR UPDATE
USING ((SELECT role FROM public.users WHERE id = auth.uid()) = 'admin' OR auth.uid() = author_id)
WITH CHECK (auth.uid() = author_id);

-- 4. Allow authors to delete their own notes
CREATE POLICY "Allow authors to delete their own notes"
ON public.notes
FOR DELETE
USING ((SELECT role FROM public.users WHERE id = auth.uid()) = 'admin' OR auth.uid() = author_id);
```

You should create similar policies for `assignments`, `drafts` (users can only access their own), `feedback`, and other tables containing sensitive data.

#### 2. Set Up Supabase Storage

The application uses Supabase Storage to handle image uploads for user avatars and notes.

1.  Go to the `Storage` section in your Supabase project.
2.  Create a **new public bucket** named `avatars`.
3.  Create another **new public bucket** named `note_images`.

Making the buckets "public" means that anyone with the direct URL can view the images, which is standard for things like profile pictures.

With this SQL script and the additional configuration, your Supabase backend will be perfectly aligned with the Class Nest application's data structure. Let me know if you have any more questions

### **SQL Update Script**

```sql
-- This script updates your existing tables to support School and Teacher features.
-- It is safe to run this script multiple times.

-- 1. Add 'school_name' to the users table
-- This column will store the name of the school for users who sign up as "School Owners".
ALTER TABLE public.users

-- 2. Add 'author_id' to the notes table
-- This links each note to the user (teacher or admin) who created it.
-- This is crucial for ensuring that coaching teachers only see the content they've created.
ALTER TABLE public.notes
ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS author_id uuid REFERENCES public.users(id) ON DELETE SET NULL;


-- 3. Add 'author_id' to the assignments table
-- This links each assignment to the user (teacher or admin) who created it,
-- serving the same purpose as the author_id in the notes table.
ALTER TABLE public.assignments
ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS author_id uuid REFERENCES public.users(id) ON DELETE SET NULL;
```

*   `ALTER TABLE public.assignments ADD COLUMN ...`: This does the same thing for the `assignments` table, ensuring assignments are also linked to their creator.
